name: Oracle
headline: Oracle CARRY-FORWARD CONFIRMATION at 28.4 / Developing (working baseline 20.6 pending application from May 16 first-baseline proposal) — May 17 evidence is non-material vs the May 16 proposal. The TechCrunch May 8 article ('Laid-off Oracle workers tried to negotiate better severance. Oracle said no.') predates the May 16 proposal and was already incorporated. WARN Act investigations remain active without resolution. No new entity-specific event on May 17. Per do-not-re-propose-unless-new-material-evidence protocol, hold the May 16 proposal in pending status. If applied: confirm at 20.6 (boundary case; 0.6pt above Critical/Developing 20/21 boundary). If not applied: apply from May 16 evidence package.
workingBaseline_note: Working baseline pending: May 16 first-baseline proposal (28.4 → 20.6) not yet applied. This May 17 proposal does NOT re-propose a change; it confirms carry-forward pending May 16 application. If applied, working baseline becomes 20.6 with boundary case status (0.6pt above Critical floor at 20.0).

evidence.summary: TechCrunch May 8: Laid-off Oracle workers tried to negotiate better severance; Oracle said no. Oracle offered 4 weeks base + 1 week/year (capped at 26 weeks) but refused RSU acceleration. Article predates May 16 proposal and was already incorporated.
Strauss Borrelli PLLC WARN Act investigations remain active: Washington (491 employees), Missouri (539 employees), both March 31 2026. Status unchanged May 17.
Amazing Workplaces commentary on Oracle layoffs 2026 HR communication lessons. Post-event analysis; no new event.
247WallSt May 7 sector sweep: pre-existing context already scored in May 16 first-baseline proposal.
May 16 first-baseline change proposal (28.4 → 20.6) pending application. Boundary case: 0.6pt above Critical/Developing boundary (20/21).

rationale: Confirm at 28.4 (working baseline 20.6 pending May 16 first-baseline application). The May 17 evidence is non-material vs May 16 proposal — the TechCrunch May 8 article was already incorporated; WARN Act investigations remain in pending status. Per protocol governing recently-proposed first-baseline entities, hold and monitor for WARN Act lawsuit outcomes. The boundary case status (0.6pt above Critical floor if applied) requires monitoring in subsequent cycles. Math: (2.2+1.8+2.2+2.0+1.8+2.2+2.4+2.4)/8 = 17.0/8 = 2.125 → ((2.125-1)/4)*100 = 28.125 → 28.1. Note: published composite is stated as 28.4; reconstructed math yields 28.1. Math-hygiene flag for dimensional-vs-composite drift (0.3pt gap).
